cases.title: Authenticated participant completes signup, login, and sensitive self-service read
preconditions: API server running at /api/v1
No existing account for test email
steps: POST /api/v1/auth/register with valid email, password, displayName (no prior session)
POST /api/v1/auth/login with the same credentials and capture JWT
cases.expected: Register and login succeed with JWT and Participant role; authenticated GET /me/registrations returns HTTP 200 with paginated envelope; sensitive self-service read succeeds only after authentication per NFR-07 and AC-16

cases.title: Authenticated participant can register for event after login
preconditions: Published event in RegistrationOpen state with available capacity
Participant account with valid JWT and no active registration for the event
cases.expected: Registration mutation succeeds with Registered state when seats available; registration-status reflects authenticated actor; sensitive registration write permitted only with valid session

cases.title: Authorization pipeline authenticates actor before role and scope validation
preconditions: Command handler wired with authorization decision pattern: authenticate → role → scope → state guard
Sensitive organizer mutation endpoint (e.g. POST /events/{eventId}/publish) available
steps: Invoke handler with valid JSON body but no authenticated actor in request context
Verify response status and that no domain mutation or audit write occurred
cases.expected: Request short-circuits at step 1 (authenticate actor) with HTTP 401; role permission and event scope validators are not reached; no publish side effects without authentication per roles-permissions authorization pattern

cases.title: Unauthenticated POST registration is rejected before domain execution
preconditions: Event in RegistrationOpen with available seats
Request omits Authorization header
steps: Query registrations table or list endpoint for new row tied to anonymous request
cases.expected: HTTP 401 unauthenticated; no registration row created; authentication gate blocks sensitive participant mutation before capacity or dedupe logic runs

cases.title: Unauthenticated organizer read of audit logs returns 401
preconditions: Event with persisted audit log entries
No Authorization header on request
GET /events/{eventId}/audit-logs is OrganizerAdmin-only per permission matrix
cases.expected: HTTP 401 unauthenticated; audit log items and actor metadata not exposed; sensitive governance read requires authentication per NFR-07

cases.title: Protected participant route blocks sensitive UI until user signs in
preconditions: Web app running with route guards enabled
Browser context has no stored JWT
steps: Navigate directly to a protected participant route (e.g. My registrations)
Observe URL and rendered content before any credentials are entered
cases.expected: User is redirected to /login; protected page content and registration data do not render; client-side session absence enforces authentication before sensitive self-service UI

cases.title: Representative sensitive write endpoints uniformly require authentication
preconditions: Seeded event in RegistrationOpen state
All requests in this scenario omit Authorization header
cases.expected: Each mutating request returns HTTP 401 (not 403, 404, or 500); no mutation side effects; authentication is required before all sensitive operations across registration, check-in, feedback, governance, and media groups per API auth principle

cases.title: Unauthenticated requests receive 401 not 403 on sensitive endpoints
preconditions: Protected endpoints available for participant and organizer operations
No Authorization header; actor identity is unknown
cases.expected: All responses are HTTP 401 unauthenticated; system does not return 403 unauthorized when actor is not authenticated; authentication precedes authorization per decision pattern step order

cases.title: Non-public API surface is protected by default per API design principle
preconditions: API mounted at /api/v1 with auth middleware on protected routes
Inventory of endpoint groups from API design (event management, registration, check-in, feedback, monitoring, media, self-service)
steps: For each documented non-public endpoint group, send a representative request without Authorization
Record HTTP status for GET /me, GET /me/registrations, POST registrations, GET dashboard, GET export
cases.expected: Every non-public endpoint returns 401 without credentials; only explicitly public auth endpoints (POST /auth/register, POST /auth/login) accept unauthenticated callers; auth-required-for-non-public principle holds across the API surface

cases.title: 401 authentication failures use standard typed error envelope
preconditions: API error middleware enabled
Unauthenticated request to protected endpoint GET /me
cases.expected: HTTP 401 with error object containing stable code, message, requestId, and details; no stack trace or sensitive profile data; immediate fail feedback per NFR-13 on auth gate rejection

cases.title: Malformed JWT bearer token blocks sensitive operation with 401
preconditions: Protected endpoint available (GET /me or POST registrations)
Authorization header set to Bearer with invalid or truncated token
steps: Attempt POST /api/v1/events/{eventId}/registrations with same malformed token
cases.expected: Both requests return HTTP 401; no profile or registration data returned; invalid token treated as unauthenticated before any sensitive operation proceeds

cases.title: Expired JWT blocks sensitive read and write until re-login
preconditions: JWT issued with past exp claim or fixed-clock test fixture
Protected endpoints GET /me/registrations and POST registrations available
cases.expected: Both requests return HTTP 401; client must re-authenticate via POST /auth/login; expired session cannot perform sensitive operations

cases.title: Public auth endpoints remain reachable without prior session
preconditions: No Authorization header on requests
Valid signup and login payload available
steps: POST /api/v1/auth/register with valid email, password, displayName
cases.expected: Both requests succeed (not 401); public auth endpoints are the only unauthenticated entry points for obtaining a session; boundary between public and protected surfaces is correct

cases.title: After sign-out, sensitive route access requires re-authentication
preconditions: User previously signed in with valid credentials
Client JWT cleared via sign-out (TopBar or equivalent)
steps: Navigate to protected participant route or trigger API call from stale client state
cases.expected: Browser redirects to /login for protected routes; API calls without refreshed token return 401; sign-out clears session so sensitive operations are blocked until re-login

cases.title: Empty Authorization bearer value is rejected before sensitive handler execution
preconditions: Protected endpoint GET /me available
steps: GET /api/v1/me with Authorization: Bearer (empty token after prefix)
cases.expected: HTTP 401 unauthenticated on both calls; equivalent to missing auth; no actor context derived and no sensitive data returned

cases.title: Sensitive organizer export and eligibility revoke require authenticated actor
preconditions: Event with registrations and eligibility records
No Authorization header
cases.expected: Both requests return HTTP 401; no export payload or eligibility mutation occurs; high-impact organizer operations gated behind authentication per NFR-07 and testing plan P0 unauthorized-critical-update severity
